Package org.apache.juneau.rest.server

Class RestOpContext

java.lang.Object
org.apache.juneau.marshall.Context
org.apache.juneau.rest.server.RestOpContext
All Implemented Interfaces:
Comparable<RestOpContext>
Direct Known Subclasses:
RrpcRestOpContext
public class RestOpContext extends Context implements Comparable<RestOpContext>
Represents a single Java servlet/resource method annotated with @RestOp.
Notes:
  • This class is thread safe and reusable.
See Also:
  • RestOpContext

    Returns the configured async-response timeout (milliseconds) for this operation, or -1 when no value was supplied at the op or resource level — in which case the default 30-second fallback applies in AsyncResponseProcessor.

  • Constructor Details

    • RestOpContext

      public RestOpContext(Method method, RestContext context) throws jakarta.servlet.ServletException
      2-arg positional context constructor.

      All operation-level configuration is resolved from RestOp-group annotations on the method and inherited from the parent RestContext.

      Parameters:
      method - The Java method this context represents. Must not be null.
      context - The owning RestContext. Must not be null.
      Throws:
      jakarta.servlet.ServletException - If context could not be created.
      Since:
      9.2.1

    • RestOpContext

      public RestOpContext(Method method, RestContext context, Supplier<Object> resourceSupplier) throws jakarta.servlet.ServletException
      3-arg positional context constructor.
      Parameters:
      method - The Java method this context represents. Must not be null.
      context - The owning RestContext. Must not be null.
      resourceSupplier - Supplier that returns the invocation target for this operation.
      Throws:
      jakarta.servlet.ServletException - If context could not be created.

  • Method Details

    • getContext

      public RestContext getContext()
      Returns the RestContext that owns this operation context.

      For operations declared directly on the host resource class, returns the host context. For operations registered through a @Rest(mixins=...) mixin, returns the per-mixin sub-context (parent-linked to the host's context via RestContext.getParentContext()). Callers can use this to identify whether the operation was contributed by a mixin (via RestContext.isMixinContext()).

      Returns:
      The owning RestContext for this operation.
      Since:
      10.0.0

    • getRestOpAnnotations

      public List<AnnotationInfo<?>> getRestOpAnnotations()
      Returns all RestOp-group annotations on this operation method, in child-to-parent order.
      Returns:
      An unmodifiable list, never null.

    • isInherited

      protected boolean isInherited(String property)
      Returns true if context-level values for the given property should be merged.
      Parameters:
      property - The annotation attribute name (e.g. RestServerConstants.PROPERTY_allowedParserOptions, RestServerConstants.PROPERTY_defaultCharset, RestServerConstants.PROPERTY_maxInput, RestServerConstants.PROPERTY_debug).
      Returns:
      true if noInherit does not contain this property.

    • getAllowedParserOptions

      public SortedSet<String> getAllowedParserOptions()
      Returns the parser session-option keys allowed for this operation.
      Returns:
      An unmodifiable case-insensitive sorted set, never null.

    • getAllowedSerializerOptions

      public SortedSet<String> getAllowedSerializerOptions()
      Returns the serializer session-option keys allowed for this operation.
      Returns:
      An unmodifiable case-insensitive sorted set, never null.

    • compareTo

      public int compareTo(RestOpContext o)
      Specified by:
      compareTo in interface Comparable<RestOpContext>

    • copy

      public Context.Builder copy()
      Description copied from class: Context
      Creates a builder from this context object.

      Builders are used to define new contexts (e.g. serializers, parsers) based on existing configurations.

      Overrides:
      copy in class Context
      Returns:
      A new Builder object.

    • createRequest

      public RestRequest createRequest(RestSession session) throws Exception
      Creates a RestRequest object based on the specified incoming HttpServletRequest object.
      Parameters:
      session - The current REST call.
      Returns:
      The wrapped request object.
      Throws:
      Exception - If any errors occur trying to interpret the request.

    • createResponse

      public RestResponse createResponse(RestSession session, RestRequest req) throws Exception
      Creates a RestResponse object based on the specified incoming HttpServletResponse object and the request returned by createRequest(RestSession).
      Parameters:
      session - The current REST call.
      req - The REST request.
      Returns:
      The wrapped response object.
      Throws:
      Exception - If any errors occur trying to interpret the request or response.

    • createSession

      public RestOpSession.Builder createSession(RestSession session) throws Exception
      Creates a new REST operation session.
      Parameters:
      session - The REST session.
      Returns:
      A new REST operation session.
      Throws:
      Exception - If op session could not be created.

      The compression encoders to use for this method.

    • getHttpMethod

      Returns the HTTP method name (e.g. "GET").
      Returns:
      The HTTP method name.

    • getJavaMethod

      public Method getJavaMethod()
      Returns the underlying Java method that this context belongs to.
      Returns:
      The underlying Java method that this context belongs to.

    • getJsonSchemaGenerator

      public JsonSchemaGenerator getJsonSchemaGenerator()
      Returns the JSON-Schema generator applicable to this Java method.
      Returns:
      The JSON-Schema generator applicable to this Java method.

    • getMaxInput

      public long getMaxInput()
      Returns the max number of bytes to process in the input content.
      Returns:
      The max number of bytes to process in the input content.

    • isProblemDetails

      public boolean isProblemDetails()
      Returns whether this operation emits RFC 7807 application/problem+json responses.

      Tri-state inheritance semantics: an explicit "true" or "false" on the operation annotation wins; otherwise the value is inherited from RestContext.isProblemDetails() (resource-level @Rest(problemDetails)).

      See Also:
      • Rest.problemDetails()
      • RestOp.problemDetails()
      Returns:
      true if RFC 7807 problem-details responses are enabled on this operation.

    • isVirtualThreadsEnabled

      public boolean isVirtualThreadsEnabled()
      Returns whether this operation opts into per-request virtual-thread dispatch on Java 21+.

      The op-level @RestOp(virtualThreads) value (when non-blank) wins; otherwise the value is inherited from RestContext.isVirtualThreadsEnabled() (resource-level @Rest(virtualThreads)). Honored only when the resource-level RestContext.getVirtualThreadExecutor() is non-null (Java 21+).

      Returns:
      true if virtual-thread dispatch is configured on this operation.

    • isObservabilityEnabled

      public boolean isObservabilityEnabled()
      Returns whether the observability block is enabled for this operation.

      Returns false when the effective resolved attribute is "false" (explicit opt-out). Returns true in all other cases ("true" strict opt-in or empty/null default).

      Note: the default behavior (empty/null attribute) still runs the observability block, but silently falls back to NoOpMetricsRecorder when no backend is wired. Only "false" completely short-circuits the block.

      Returns:
      false when this operation has explicitly opted out of observability.
      See Also:
      • RestOp.observability()
      • Rest.observability()

    • getMetricName

      public String getMetricName()
      Returns the per-op metric name override from @RestOp(metricName).
      Returns:
      The metric name override, or an empty string when not set.
      See Also:
      • RestOp.metricName()

    • getMetricTags

      public String getMetricTags()
      Returns the per-op additional metric tags from @RestOp(metricTags).
      Returns:
      The tags string (comma-separated key=value pairs), or empty string when not set.
      See Also:
      • RestOp.metricTags()

    • getAsyncTimeoutMillis

      public long getAsyncTimeoutMillis()
      Returns the configured async-response timeout (milliseconds) for this operation, or -1 when no value was supplied at the op or resource level — in which case the default 30-second fallback applies in AsyncResponseProcessor.
      Returns:
      The async timeout in milliseconds, or -1 when unset.

    • getAsyncCompletionExecutor

      public Executor getAsyncCompletionExecutor()
      Returns the effective Executor for routing CompletableFuture completion callbacks on this operation, or null when neither the op nor the resource configures an executor (natural completion thread).
      Returns:
      The executor, or null if no async-completion executor is configured.
      Since:
      10.0.0

      Returns the parsers to use for this method.

      The parsers to use for this method.

    • hashCode

      public int hashCode()
      Overrides:
      hashCode in class Object

    • match

      protected int match(RestSession session)
      Identifies if this method can process the specified call.

      To process the call, the following must be true:

      • Path pattern must match.
      • Matchers (if any) must match.
      Parameters:
      session - The call to check.
      Returns:
      One of the following values:
      • 0 - Path doesn't match.
      • 1 - Path matched but matchers did not.
      • 2 - Matches.

    • properties

      protected FluentMap<String,Object> properties()
      Description copied from class: Context
      Returns the properties on this bean as a map for debugging.
      Overrides:
      properties in class Context
      Returns:
      The properties on this bean as a map for debugging.
